findings_details.title: Regex in defaultRandomSuffix test hardcodes {4} instead of using RANDOM_SUFFIX_LEN
findings_details.body: Line 46 uses /^[0-9a-f]{4}$/ with a hardcoded 4, but RANDOM_SUFFIX_LEN is imported and checked on line 47. If RANDOM_SUFFIX_LEN changes to any even value other than 4 (e.g. 6 or 8), the regex silently passes while the length assertion on line 47 catches the mismatch — meaning the test gives a false impression that the regex is the authoritative check. The regex should mirror the constant: new RegExp('^[0-9a-f]{' + RANDOM_SUFFIX_LEN + '}$')

findings_details.title: RANDOM_SUFFIX_LEN / 2 byte allocation silently truncates if constant is ever set to an odd value
findings_details.body: Line 32: new Uint8Array(RANDOM_SUFFIX_LEN / 2). TypedArray constructors truncate fractional lengths via ToIndex (ECMAScript), so RANDOM_SUFFIX_LEN=5 would allocate 2 bytes and produce only 4 hex chars instead of 5, while the constant says 5. No current bug (value is 4), but the contract is fragile. A safer formulation is Math.ceil(RANDOM_SUFFIX_LEN / 2) bytes then slice(0, RANDOM_SUFFIX_LEN) on the hex string, or add a compile-time assertion that RANDOM_SUFFIX_LEN % 2 === 0.

findings_details.title: RANDOM_SUFFIX_LEN / 2 silently truncates if constant is set to an odd number
findings_details.body: Line 32: `new Uint8Array(RANDOM_SUFFIX_LEN / 2)`. TypedArray constructors in JavaScript silently floor non-integer sizes — if RANDOM_SUFFIX_LEN were ever changed to an odd value (e.g. 5), the array would be 2 bytes and produce only 4 hex chars, not 5. The constant name would then be a lie and the mismatch would be invisible at runtime. Fix: replace the division with an explicit byte count constant, or assert that RANDOM_SUFFIX_LEN is even, or document the invariant with a comment: `// 2 bytes → 4 hex chars; RANDOM_SUFFIX_LEN must be even`.

findings_details.title: Trailing-dash-strip test does not actually trigger the code path it claims to test
findings_details.body: The test 'strips trailing dash before appending HHMM and random suffix' (line 108) uses slug = 'a'.repeat(46) + '-extra'. The computed base is 'issue-1-' (8 chars) + 46 a's + '-extra' (6 chars) = 60 chars. With suffix '-0905-abcd' (10 chars), baseMax = 50. Slicing base to 50 chars yields 'issue-1-' + 42 a's — no trailing dash, so `.replace(/-+$/, '')` is a no-op. The assertion `expect(result).not.toMatch(/--\d{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}$/)` passes trivially and does not exercise the dash-stripping branch. Fix: use a slug whose truncation point lands on a dash, e.g. slug = 'a'.repeat(41) + '-b' so that base.slice(0, 50) ends with a '-', confirming the replace actually fires.
